title: Executive Order 9511—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Properties of the Cleveland Electric Illuminating Company, Located in and Around Cleveland, Ohio

flags.justification: 'In such manner as he deems necessary for the successful prosecution of the War' — broad, open discretion with no external check.
A concrete 60-day cap on possession is set, tied to the Secretary's own determination that possession is no longer necessary.
Single purpose: seizure of this one utility's properties.

'In such manner as he deems necessary' has no defined limits, and unlike several sibling orders in this batch, this one includes no status-quo-employment-terms constraint or specific directive-order compliance requirement.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
The 60-day cap is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap provides a real, checkable structural constraint even without additional specific operating conditions.
A routine application of the established wartime plant-seizure template.
No other agency's mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 2/20 = 10.0%. One of the sparest, single-paragraph seizure orders in the corpus — has the standard 60-day cap but lacks the status-quo-employment-terms or specific-directive-compliance language found in several sibling orders.
